skill.name: remotion-render
skill.description: Render React and Remotion TSX components into MP4 videos through the inference.sh CLI, with configurable duration, FPS, resolution, props, and codec.
skill.summary: Turn Remotion component code into MP4 video renders through inference.sh.
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified external command usage and network references because the skill instructs agents to run the inference.sh CLI and submit Remotion code for remote rendering. The weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives from product text, and no prompt injection or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill authorizes and documents Bash execution of infsh commands. This is core functionality, but it means agents may run external commands that submit rendering jobs and consume account resources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration limits execution to infsh, and multiple examples invoke infsh directly. The usage is intentional and documented, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malware.

medium_findings.title: Remote Rendering of User-Provided TSX
medium_findings.description: The skill sends TSX component code and props to an external rendering service. Users should avoid including secrets, private data, or untrusted code in render inputs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly describes rendering through inference.sh and shows user-supplied code in CLI input. There is no evidence of hidden exfiltration, but remote submission is a real privacy boundary.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to inference.sh, Remotion documentation, and a hosted image used by the README. These links are expected documentation references, not hidden network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links and image references. They do not execute by themselves, but they disclose external dependencies.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto Static Finding Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings are false positives. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive metadata and a Markdown table header, not cryptographic operations. The scanner appears to have matched substrings in ordinary documentation text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Access for infsh
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill permits Bash execution for infsh commands. This should remain narrowly scoped and should not be expanded without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools line is direct evidence of permitted command execution. The scope is narrow, which reduces but does not remove risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Service Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples invoke infsh app run against infsh/remotion-render. This sends render requests to the inference.sh service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands and SDK examples target a named remote app. The behavior is expected, but it is still a networked execution pattern.

content.value_statement: Programmatic video rendering often requires build setup and manual export steps. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code send Remotion TSX to inference.sh and receive MP4 output.
actual_capabilities: Runs inference.sh CLI commands for the infsh/remotion-render app.
Accepts React and Remotion TSX code that exports a default component.
Passes optional props into the rendered component.
Configures width, height, FPS, duration, composition ID, and codec.
Shows Python SDK usage for normal and streaming render jobs.
References Remotion APIs such as AbsoluteFill, Sequence, spring, and interpolate.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI or SDK and an authenticated account.
Submits render inputs to an external hosted service.
Does not include local rendering or sandbox implementation code.
Output quality depends on valid Remotion code and service availability.

use_cases.title: Create branded motion graphics
use_cases.description: Generate short MP4 animations from React components for announcements, product clips, or social posts.

use_cases.title: Automate data-driven videos
use_cases.description: Render repeatable video templates with changing props, numbers, labels, or visual states.

use_cases.title: Prototype Remotion scenes
use_cases.description: Test simple Remotion component ideas through a remote render flow before building a local pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Render a simple title card
prompt_templates.prompt: Use remotion-render to create a 3 second MP4 title card with centered text, 1080p resolution, and 30 FPS.

prompt_templates.title: Render with custom props
prompt_templates.prompt: Render a Remotion component that accepts title, subtitle, and brandColor props. Use the provided values and return the video output.

prompt_templates.title: Create a sequenced animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Build and render a Remotion scene with three timed Sequence sections, smooth fade transitions, and square 1080 by 1080 output.

prompt_templates.title: Stream a longer render
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Python SDK streaming example to render a 10 second Remotion animation and report progress until the MP4 URL is available.

output_examples.input: A 3 second fade-in welcome title at 1920 by 1080 and 30 FPS.
output_examples.output: The skill submits the TSX component to inference.sh and returns a completed MP4 video location.

output_examples.input: A square animated counter that counts from 0 to 100 over 5 seconds.
output_examples.output: The skill renders a 1080 by 1080 MP4 with the counter animation and selected frame rate.

output_examples.input: A Remotion template with title and subtitle props for a product update.
output_examples.output: The skill passes the props to the component and returns the rendered video output.

best_practices: Review TSX inputs before rendering because they are sent to a remote service.
Keep durations and resolutions reasonable while testing to control render time and cost.
Use props for reusable text and data instead of rewriting whole components.
anti_patterns: Do not include secrets, tokens, or private customer data in component code or props.
Do not render code from untrusted users without separate review and sandbox controls.
Do not assume the skill can render without inference.sh authentication.

faq.question: What does this skill render?
faq.answer: It renders React and Remotion TSX components into MP4 videos through inference.sh.

faq.question: Does it run locally?
faq.answer: No. The documented flow uses the inference.sh CLI or Python SDK to call a remote app.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: Can I set video dimensions?
faq.answer: Yes. The input schema includes width, height, FPS, duration, codec, props, and composition ID.

faq.question: Is it safe to include secrets?
faq.answer: No. Do not include secrets or private data in code or props sent for rendering.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs external CLI commands and sends code to a hosted service, but no malicious behavior was found.
